title: Gen AI · Ground
subtitle: Branch 4 — vectors, chunks, embed, Chroma · in-memory vs persist

nodes.label: Why chunk at all
nodes.definition: Whole policy file won't fit in one prompt slot; retrieval needs smaller searchable pieces.
nodes.whyItMatters: You saw 16 chunks from one .txt — retrieval returns top-k of those, not the full file.
nodes.safeSentence: Chunking = how big is each searchable piece.

nodes.label: RECIPE · CharacterTextSplitter (now)
nodes.definition: chunk_size=1000, overlap=0 in m1_rag_shared — course M1 default.
nodes.whyItMatters: Oversized chunk warnings are a splitter quirk — not fatal.
nodes.safeSentence: TextLoader → CharacterTextSplitter → list of Document objects.

note.summary:
Your settings today:
• chunk_size 1000
• overlap 0
• warnings when a split lands at ~1600–2000 chars

nodes.label: LATER · overlap & other splitters
nodes.definition: RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter, semantic chunking — industry path; not required for M1 PASS.
nodes.whyItMatters: You deferred this — map slot reserved so you know where it will live.
nodes.safeSentence: When header-only chunks hurt answers, come back here.

nodes.label: Chroma · in-memory (M1)
nodes.definition: Chroma.from_documents(...) with no persist_directory — dies when script ends.
nodes.whyItMatters: All your PASS scripts so far. Re-ingest each run (cached .txt on disk, vectors in RAM).
nodes.safeSentence: Fast to learn; rebuild each session.

nodes.label: Chroma · persist (later)
nodes.definition: persist_directory=... — embeddings survive between runs; re-ingest only when doc changes.
nodes.whyItMatters: Slot on the map now so you do not confuse it with in-memory when course introduces it.
nodes.safeSentence: Same Chroma API; add a folder path when ready.

note.summary:
Not in your M1 scripts yet.
When you add it: new recipe bubble under this node.

nodes.label: Embeddings · text-embedding-3-small
nodes.definition: Embedding model turns each chunk into a vector — same credentials pattern as the LLM client.
nodes.whyItMatters: Swap embed model → must re-ingest (vectors change).
nodes.safeSentence: Chunks become vectors here; Chroma stores them.

nodes.label: INSPECT · return_source_documents
nodes.definition: See which chunks the retriever actually picked — top-k only.
nodes.whyItMatters: Lab 05 — header chunk vs body chunk. Chunking issue, not retrieval bug.
